entries.notes: Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Learn page documents Game Mode as a real Windows feature area. The record uses it only for feature-level proof, not for the AutoGameModeEnabled registry mapping.

Manual review confirmed the support page directly documents the fullscreen/windowed-games feature area and user-facing toggle path. The record does not claim the page publishes the raw GameConfigStore tuple.

Manual review confirmed the support material is used only for the user-facing Restart apps sign-in feature. The record relies on VM runtime proof for the RestartApps registry mapping, not on the support page for raw registry semantics.
Manual review confirmed the Win32_OperatingSystem page is used narrowly for the path plus high-level WMI mapping to Win32PrioritySeparation, not for raw 0x26 bit semantics.
Manual review confirmed the MMCSS page is used only for the SystemResponsiveness path plus rounding/clamping behavior. It is not used as proof of NetworkThrottlingIndex semantics.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof points only to the SystemResponsiveness path plus rounding/clamping portion of the MMCSS page.

Manual review confirmed the Microsoft Q&A article contains the same add/delete CLSID workaround commands used in the record. The record still treats the workaround as potentially deprecated on newer 24H2-era builds.
Manual review confirmed the VS Code default settings reference is being used only to prove the documented git.autofetch settings surface, not the app's unrelated Explorer registry write.

Manual review confirmed the Source Control FAQ explicitly discusses disabling autofetch through git.autofetch. The record uses this page for the VS Code setting only, not for the app's registry mapping.
Manual review confirmed the FAQ line about git.autofetch matches the validation proof claim in this deprecated record.

Manual review confirmed Microsoft's DirectX post directly documents Hardware-Accelerated GPU Scheduling as a real Windows graphics feature and user-facing settings area. The record uses it for feature-level proof, not as the raw HwSchMode registry contract.

Manual review confirmed Docker's settings page documents the Windows settings-store.json location. The record uses this page for the supported file-backed settings surface rather than for registry semantics.
Manual review confirmed Docker's settings reference documents wslEngineEnabled as the WSL 2 engine setting. The record uses it for the documented file-backed Docker settings model.
Manual review confirmed the settings reference includes the WSL engine wording cited in the validation proof.

Manual review confirmed Python's Windows documentation explicitly points readers to Enable Win32 long paths or LongPathsEnabled=1 in the FileSystem registry key. The record uses this page for the Python workflow context.
Manual review confirmed the exact quote in the validation proof matches the current Python Windows documentation wording about LongPathsEnabled.
Manual review confirmed Microsoft directly documents LongPathsEnabled under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem and states that value 1 enables long paths for compatible applications.
Manual review confirmed the validation proof quote matches the current Microsoft path-length documentation for LongPathsEnabled.
Manual review confirmed Microsoft's long-path page is a correct exact-path and exact-value source for LongPathsEnabled in this record.
Manual review confirmed Microsoft's long-path documentation matches this record's narrow claim: LongPathsEnabled is the documented Windows control for enabling long paths for compatible applications.
Manual review confirmed this note references the same Microsoft long-path page in a way that matches the page content.
